blocking_issues.title: NodeSpec test fixtures will TypeError — 7 required fields missing
blocking_issues.plan_says: Tests construct NodeSpec(id=..., node_type=..., plugin=..., input=..., on_success=..., options={})
blocking_issues.codebase_has: NodeSpec is a @dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True). Required fields (no defaults): id, node_type, plugin, input, on_success, on_error, options, condition, routes, fork_to, branches, policy, merge (13 fields). Only trigger, output_mode, expected_output_count have defaults (lines 142-144 of src/elspeth/web/composer/state.py). The plan's 6-arg call is missing on_error, condition, routes, fork_to, branches, policy, merge.
blocking_issues.fix: Pass all required NodeSpec kwargs: on_error=None, condition=None, routes=None, fork_to=None, branches=None, policy=None, merge=None.

blocking_issues.title: OutputSpec test fixtures will TypeError — on_write_failure required
blocking_issues.codebase_has: OutputSpec has 4 required fields: name, plugin, options, on_write_failure (src/elspeth/web/composer/state.py:255-258). No defaults. The plan's 3-arg call is missing on_write_failure.
blocking_issues.fix: Add on_write_failure='discard' (or appropriate value) to all OutputSpec constructions.

blocking_issues.title: error_code and affected_nodes defaults are a No-Legacy policy violation
blocking_issues.plan_says: Add error_code: str | None = None to ValidationError and affected_nodes: tuple[str, ...] = () to ValidationCheck. 'default keeps existing call sites valid' and '_build_provenance_row will produce empty component_ids on old check instances until the advisory code is updated.'
blocking_issues.codebase_has: ValidationCheck currently has: name: str, passed: bool, detail: str (no affected_nodes). ValidationError has: component_id, component_type, message, suggestion (no error_code). CLAUDE.md No-Legacy policy: 'Change all call sites in the same commit.' The plan openly defers the call-site update, explicitly relying on the default as a backwards-compat shim. The phrase 'until the advisory code is updated' is a deferred breaking change — forbidden.
blocking_issues.evidence: src/elspeth/web/execution/schemas.py:50-64; /home/john/elspeth/CLAUDE.md 'No Legacy Code Policy' section; 14a §File structure: 'default keeps existing call sites valid'
blocking_issues.fix: Update all existing call sites that construct ValidationCheck and ValidationError in the same commit that adds the new fields. Search with: grep -rn 'ValidationCheck(' src/ tests/ --include='*.py'. Do not ship the new fields with defaults that silently produce wrong results on old instances.

blocking_issues.title: api/preferences.ts does not exist — precedent file is phantom
blocking_issues.plan_says: 14b Task 2 Step 1: 'The Phase 1B preferences client is the precedent (api/preferences.ts). Mirror that file's structure.'
blocking_issues.codebase_has: No api/preferences.ts file exists. The preferences API functions (fetchUserComposerPreferences, patchUserComposerPreferences) are implemented directly in src/elspeth/web/frontend/src/api/client.ts at lines 407+. The file listing confirms: client.guided.test.ts, client.preferences.test.ts, client.recovery.test.ts, client.ts, websocket.ts — no preferences.ts.
blocking_issues.fix: The precedent is the inline functions in client.ts (getToken, authHeaders, parseResponse are private there; the preferences GET/PATCH are public exports). Update Step 1 to reference client.ts:407 as the structural precedent, not a non-existent file.

blocking_issues.title: EXTERNAL_BOUNDARY_TRANSFORMS allowlist has 3 non-existent plugin names
blocking_issues.codebase_has: Plugin name grep confirms: 'llm' EXISTS (transforms/llm/transform.py:1039). 'web_fetch' — NOT FOUND anywhere. 'web_scrape_extraction' — NOT FOUND as a plugin name; the file plugins/transforms/web_scrape_extraction.py exists but it exports extract_content(), not a plugin class. The transform plugin for web scraping is 'web_scrape' (transforms/web_scrape.py:339). 'dataverse_query' — NOT FOUND; the Dataverse plugin exists only as a SINK named 'dataverse' (sinks/dataverse.py:224).
blocking_issues.evidence: grep -rn 'name = "' src/elspeth/plugins/transforms/ — output lists: llm, web_scrape (not web_scrape_extraction), passthrough, etc. No web_fetch, no web_scrape_extraction, no dataverse_query. sinks/dataverse.py:224: name = 'dataverse'.
blocking_issues.note: The plan's own subset-of-catalog test (test_external_boundary_transforms_subset_of_catalog) will fail immediately on first run with these 3 entries. Additionally, EXTERNAL_CALL transforms found in codebase that are NOT on the allowlist: web_scrape (Determinism.EXTERNAL_CALL), rag_retrieval (Determinism.EXTERNAL_CALL), azure_content_safety (Determinism.EXTERNAL_CALL via base), azure_prompt_shield (Determinism.EXTERNAL_CALL via base). The completeness test (test_every_external_call_plugin_is_on_allowlist_or_explicitly_excepted) will also fail.
blocking_issues.fix: Replace the allowlist with verified names: 'llm' (NON_DETERMINISTIC not EXTERNAL_CALL — see transforms/llm/transform.py:1043), 'web_scrape' (EXTERNAL_CALL). Then decide whether rag_retrieval, azure_content_safety, azure_prompt_shield need adding. Drop web_fetch, web_scrape_extraction, dataverse_query entirely. Note: 'llm' has determinism=NON_DETERMINISTIC not EXTERNAL_CALL, so the completeness test won't flag it. Verify each name's determinism before populating the allowlist.

warnings.title: secret_service attribute disambiguation needed in app.py wiring
warnings.plan_says: Wire ReadinessService with secret_service=app.state.secret_service (adapt to actual attr name).
warnings.codebase_has: Two distinct attributes exist: app.state.secret_service (WebSecretService, requires auth_provider_type kwarg in list_refs), and app.state.scoped_secret_resolver (ScopedSecretResolver, 1-arg list_refs). Plan's _SecretServiceLike protocol specifies list_refs(user_id, *, auth_provider_type: str) which matches WebSecretService. The ExecutionService (line 232 in app.py) receives app.state.scoped_secret_resolver. Plan's run_sync_in_worker call passes auth_provider_type= as kwarg, which is correct for WebSecretService. An implementer could plausibly reach for scoped_secret_resolver and then break the kwargs call.
warnings.fix: Confirm in plan comment that the intent is app.state.secret_service (WebSecretService) NOT app.state.scoped_secret_resolver. The plan's _SecretServiceLike protocol signature aligns with WebSecretService.

warnings.title: getInitialState claim: executionStore does NOT export this function
warnings.plan_says: 14b Task 3: 'getInitialState is exported as a named const (not via an ad-hoc cast), so tests can import it directly. The reset() action calls set(getInitialState()) — same pattern used by executionStore.'
warnings.codebase_has: executionStore.ts:235 defines 'const initialExecutionState = {…}' as a module-internal const — NOT exported, NOT named getInitialState. The reset() at line 530 calls set(initialExecutionState). The plan's auditReadinessStore uses an exported getInitialState() function — this is a NEW pattern, not a mirror of executionStore.
warnings.fix: Remove the 'same pattern used by executionStore' claim. auditReadinessStore's exported getInitialState() is a legitimate improvement but is novel in this codebase. The footprint is fine; the citation is misleading.

warnings.title: Line number claims are slightly off for two references
warnings.plan_says: 14a Task 5 Step 1: 'execution_service confirmed in execution/routes.py:80'. 14a Task 1 Step 2: 'add after existing payload_store_path field (around line 99)'. 14b Task 2: 'validatePipeline (line 702)'.
warnings.codebase_has: execution_service accessed at routes.py:80 (correct — cast on line 80). payload_store_path at config.py:99 (correct — exact). validatePipeline at client.ts:730 (plan says 702 — off by 28 lines). catalog_router inclusion at app.py:388 (plan says 'around line 386' — close enough).
warnings.evidence: grep validatePipeline src/elspeth/web/frontend/src/api/client.ts → line 730; grep catalog_router src/elspeth/web/app.py → line 388
warnings.fix: Update the validatePipeline line reference from 702 to 730 in 14b Task 2. Minor but could cause grep confusion.

warnings.title: Integration test fixture names are entirely novel — no scaffolding exists
warnings.plan_says: 14a Task 5 Step 2: 'Adapt fixture names below' for client_with_session, client_anonymous, client_with_session_without_state, client_with_user, other_users_session_id, any_session_id.
warnings.codebase_has: tests/integration/web/conftest.py defines: composer_test_client, session_with_pending_compose_request, session_with_composer_state, inject_commit_OperationalError. None of the plan's expected fixture names exist. client_anonymous appears only in test_preferences_routes.py as a local fixture. The plan acknowledges adaptation, but the gap is larger than the note implies — 6 fixtures need to be created from scratch.
warnings.fix: The plan's 'adapt fixture names' note is correct guidance but understates the work. The implementer must create all 6 fixtures before writing the integration tests. The composer_test_client fixture is the starting point; model the auth/ownership fixtures on it.

warnings.title: subscriptions.ts does not yet fire validation side effects — Task 8 sequencing risk
warnings.plan_says: 14c Task 8: 'Delete handleValidate and callers (injectSystemMessage, sendValidationFeedback) from InspectorPanel.tsx. The side effects belong to subscriptions.ts — Phase 3A Task 8 already does this relocation.'
warnings.codebase_has: subscriptions.ts currently contains only auto-clear validation on compositionState.version change (line 18: 'Auto-clear validation when compositionState.version changes'). The injectSystemMessage/sendValidationFeedback wiring is NOT present. Phase 3A Task 8 is not yet landed. Deleting handleValidate before Phase 3A Task 8 ships silently drops the validation-failure chat feedback flow.
warnings.fix: Cross-link the dependency explicitly: 14c Task 8 MUST be sequenced after Phase 3A Task 8 lands its subscription wiring, OR the subscription wiring must be co-landed in 14c (expanding scope). As-written, 14c silently breaks the validation-chat feedback loop.

warnings.title: llm plugin determinism is NON_DETERMINISTIC, not EXTERNAL_CALL
warnings.plan_says: test_every_external_call_plugin_is_on_allowlist_or_explicitly_excepted checks plugins with Determinism.EXTERNAL_CALL. The allowlist includes 'llm'.
warnings.codebase_has: transforms/llm/transform.py:1043: determinism: Determinism = Determinism.NON_DETERMINISTIC. The 'llm' plugin is not EXTERNAL_CALL. Including it in EXTERNAL_BOUNDARY_TRANSFORMS is semantically correct for audit purposes (it makes external API calls) but the completeness test keyed on Determinism.EXTERNAL_CALL will NOT catch 'llm' as an uncovered gap — and the subset test will fail if 'llm' is on the allowlist with the wrong casing or name mismatch.
warnings.fix: The 'llm' plugin name is correct. But clarify in the plan that the subset-of-catalog test verifies the name exists; the completeness test (EXTERNAL_CALL) will not cover 'llm' because its determinism is NON_DETERMINISTIC. The two allowlist rationales are: (a) EXTERNAL_CALL → automated coverage; (b) manual curation for LLM-class plugins with NON_DETERMINISTIC. Document the two-tier approach.

warnings.title: Frontend ValidationError type lacks error_code — both sides need updating
warnings.plan_says: 14a adds error_code: str | None = None to backend ValidationError. 14b's type mirror is described for audit-readiness types only.
warnings.codebase_has: frontend/src/types/index.ts:310-315: ValidationError interface has component_id, component_type, message, suggestion — no error_code. The plan adds error_code to the backend Pydantic model. The frontend type must be widened in the same commit or the ReadinessService tests that pass ValidationError(error_code='missing_secret_ref') will work on the backend but the frontend ApiError type diverges from the actual wire shape.
warnings.fix: Add error_code?: string to the frontend ValidationError interface in types/index.ts as part of Phase 2A (backend) or Phase 2B (frontend foundations, Task 1). It is not in the current plan's file-structure section.

blocking_summary: B1: NodeSpec fixtures missing 7 required fields — all backend tests that construct _state() will TypeError before any assertion.
B2: OutputSpec fixtures missing on_write_failure required field — same _state() helpers fail.
B3: error_code/affected_nodes defaults are a No-Legacy policy violation — plan explicitly defers call-site updates, relying on defaults as a backwards-compat shim.
B4: api/preferences.ts does not exist — the cited 'precedent file' is phantom. Preferences functions are in client.ts.

B5: EXTERNAL_BOUNDARY_TRANSFORMS allowlist has 3 non-existent names (web_fetch, web_scrape_extraction, dataverse_query). The plan's own subset-of-catalog test will fail on first run. Actual EXTERNAL_CALL transforms not on the list: web_scrape, rag_retrieval, azure_content_safety, azure_prompt_shield.
summary.most_critical_path: Fix B1+B2 together (both are in the same _state() helper). Fix B3 by auditing ValidationCheck/ValidationError call sites. Fix B4 by correcting the Step 1 reconnaissance instruction. Fix B5 by running the actual grep the plan prescribes in Task 2 Step 2 before populating trust.py.
